title: Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea

seo.title: How to Make Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea recipe
authentic Mexican Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea

seo.h1: Authentic Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea Recipe
h2Headings: What is Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Agua de Jamaica, or Hibiscus Tea, is a vibrant and refreshing beverage beloved across Mexico, known for its beautiful ruby-red color and tart, floral flavor. Made from dried hibiscus flowers, this traditional drink is a perfect balance of sweet and tangy, offering a cooling respite on a warm day. It is a staple at taquerias and family gatherings, a true taste of Mexican hospitality.

instructions.title: Rinse the Hibiscus Flowers
instructions.text: Gently rinse the dried hibiscus flowers under cold water in a colander to remove any dust or impurities. This ensures a clean, pure flavor for your tea.

instructions.title: Boil and Steep
instructions.text: In a large pot, bring 4 cups of water to a rolling boil. Add the rinsed hibiscus flowers, reduce the heat to low, and let them simmer for 10-15 minutes. The water will visibly turn a deep ruby red as the flavor infuses.

instructions.title: Strain the Concentrate
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully strain the hibiscus mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a heat-proof pitcher or bowl, pressing on the flowers with a spoon to extract all the liquid. Discard the spent flowers.

instructions.title: Sweeten the Concentrate
instructions.text: While the concentrate is still warm, add 1/2 cup of granulated sugar and stir until it completely dissolves. Taste the mixture and add more sugar, up to 1 cup, if you prefer a sweeter drink. Remember, it will be diluted later.

instructions.title: Dilute the Tea
instructions.text: Add the remaining 4 cups of cold water to the hibiscus concentrate. Stir well to combine all the flavors. For a stronger flavor, you can add slightly less water; for a lighter taste, add more, adjusting to your preference.

instructions.title: Chill and Serve
instructions.text: Place the Agua de Jamaica in the refrigerator to chill thoroughly for at least 30 minutes, or until very cold. Serve generously over plenty of ice, garnished with fresh lime slices if desired for an extra zesty touch.

tips: For a richer, more complex flavor, lightly toast the dried hibiscus flowers in a dry pan for a minute or two before boiling, being careful not to burn them.
Adjust the amount of sugar to your liking; some prefer it very tart, others sweeter. You can also experiment with natural sweeteners like honey or agave nectar.
Agua de Jamaica is best served very cold over ice. For an extra refreshing kick, add a few mint leaves or a slice of orange to your glass.
article: Agua de Jamaica Hibiscus Tea is a beloved Mexican Beverages dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
